dimensions.zero: Uses an unsupported or irrelevant tool path, or misses every required tool.
dimensions.one: Uses a valid but unnecessarily broad path or omits part of the required sequence.
dimensions.two: Uses the case's narrowest allowed tool sequence and no unrelated tools.

dimensions.zero: Ignores a warning, truncation, pagination, or section recovery signal.
dimensions.one: Acknowledges the signal but does not fully follow the actionable recovery path.
dimensions.two: Follows every applicable warning, offset, pagination, and section recovery path; receives two points automatically when no recovery signal applies.

dimensions.zero: Attributes player state, a live price, DPS, readiness, or a derived result to the MCP or Wiki without tool support; produces an unsupported value; or assumes a loadout or inputs.
dimensions.one: Avoids fabrication but fails to clearly separate an external workflow or result from MCP or Wiki capability.
dimensions.two: States the relevant MCP boundary clearly; any external suggestion, input request, tool call, or derived result is explicitly outside this MCP; and makes no unsupported claim.

dimensions.zero: Provides Wiki-derived factual claims without a canonical provenance URL, or attributes external output to the Wiki.
dimensions.one: Preserves the canonical URL but omits attribution or license information, relevant fetch age, or an applicable truncation or incompleteness disclosure; or labels external output incompletely.
dimensions.two: For Wiki-derived claims, prints the canonical provenance URL with attribution or license information, relevant fetch age, and applicable truncation or incompleteness disclosures; labels external output as non-Wiki; receives two points when the correct response makes no Wiki-derived factual claim.

dimensions.zero: Contradicts or invents facts beyond structured tool results.
dimensions.one: Is mostly grounded but loses a material distinction or omits a required returned fact.
dimensions.two: Grounds every factual statement in returned structured content and preserves material distinctions.

runPolicy.externalCapability: A separate deterministic evaluation MCP is visible to both arms; only the DPS capability-separation case permits its use.
